subject: Python version support
truth: Stop using Python 3.7, 3.8, or 3.9; Jinja2 3.2.0 requires Python 3.10+.
quote: Drop support for Python 3.7, 3.8, and 3.9.

subject: Minimum MarkupSafe version
new: MarkupSafe >= 3.0
truth: Jinja2 3.2.0 requires MarkupSafe >= 3.0; older MarkupSafe versions will cause install/import failures.
quote: Update minimum MarkupSafe version to >= 3.0.

subject: Minimum Babel version
new: Babel >= 2.17
truth: Jinja2 3.2.0 requires Babel >= 2.17; older Babel versions will cause install/import failures.
quote: Update minimum Babel version to >= 2.17.

subject: Python 3.6 support
truth: Python 3.6 is no longer supported; upgrade to Python 3.7+.
quote: Drop support for Python 3.6. :pr:`1534`

subject: Undefined behavior with 'in' operator
old: Undefined acted like StrictUndefined for the 'in' operator
new: Undefined no longer acts like StrictUndefined for the 'in' operator
truth: Stop relying on Undefined raising an error when used with the 'in' operator; it will now return false instead of raising.
quote: an undefined object now instead of raising an index error.

from_fact: undefined acted like strictundefined for the 'in' operator
undefined no longer acts like strictundefined for the 'in' operator

subject: Python 2.7 support
truth: Stop using Python 2.7; Jinja2 no longer supports Python 2.7 or 3.5.
quote: Drop support for Python 2.7 and 3.5.

subject: Python 3.5 support
truth: Stop using Python 3.5; Jinja2 no longer supports Python 3.5.
quote: Drop support for Python 2.7 and 3.5.

subject: MarkupSafe minimum version
new: MarkupSafe >=1.1
truth: MarkupSafe dependency must now be at least version 1.1.
quote: Bump MarkupSafe dependency to >=1.1.

subject: Babel minimum version
new: Babel >=2.1
truth: Babel optional dependency must now be at least version 2.1.
quote: Bump Babel optional dependency to >=2.1.

subject: Deprecated code
truth: All code that was previously marked as deprecated has been removed.
quote: Remove code that was marked deprecated.

subject: Function and filter decorator names
new: Renamed and unified decorator names
truth: The function and filter decorators have been renamed and unified; old names are deprecated.
quote: The function and filter decorators have been renamed and unified. The old names are deprecated.

subject: Python versions
truth: Stop using Python 2.6, 3.3, and 3.4; they are no longer supported.
quote: Drop support for Python 2.6, 3.3, and 3.4.

subject: finalize function behavior
old: finalize was applied to all template output including static data
new: finalize is only applied to output of expressions, not static template data
truth: Stop relying on finalize being called for static template data; it now only applies to expression output.
quote: The environment's finalize function is only applied to the output of expressions (constant or not), not static template data.

from_fact: finalize was applied to all template output including static data
finalize is only applied to output of expressions, not static template data
associative: finalize not called for static text

subject: wordwrap filter handling of existing newlines
old: wordwrap treated existing newlines as part of the line to wrap, creating short intermediate lines
new: wordwrap treats existing newlines as separate paragraphs to be wrapped individually
truth: Stop expecting wordwrap to wrap across existing newlines; each paragraph separated by newlines is now wrapped independently.
quote: |wordwrap filter treats existing newlines as separate paragraphs to be wrapped individually, rather than creating short intermediate lines.

from_fact: wordwrap treated existing newlines as part of the line to wrap, creating short intermediate lines
wordwrap treats existing newlines as separate paragraphs to be wrapped individually

subject: chained comparison evaluation at compile time
old: chained comparisons of constants evaluated only the last comparison
new: chained comparisons of constants return False if any comparison returns False, matching Python behavior
truth: Stop expecting chained constant comparisons to return only the last comparison's result; they now return False if any comparison is False.
quote: When chained comparisons of constants are evaluated at compile time, the result follows Python's behavior of returning False if any comparison returns False, rather than only the last one.

from_fact: chained comparisons of constants evaluated only the last comparison
chained comparisons of constants return false if any comparison returns false, matching python behavior

subject: Undefined in get_template/extends/import/include
old: passing Undefined to get_template (via extends, import, include) could behave inconsistently
new: passing Undefined to get_template raises UndefinedError consistently; select_template shows the undefined message
truth: Stop relying on passing Undefined values to get_template/extends/import/include working silently; it now consistently raises UndefinedError.
quote: Passing an Undefined value to get_template (such as through extends, import, or include), raises an UndefinedError consistently. select_template will show the undefined message in the list of attempts rather than the empty string.

from_fact: passing undefined to get_template (via extends, import, include) could behave inconsistently
passing undefined to get_template raises undefinederror consistently; select_template shows the undefined message

subject: random filter
old: random filter was constant-folded (same value each render)
new: random filter produces a new random choice each render
truth: The random filter is no longer constant folded and will produce a new random choice each time the template is rendered.
quote: will produce a new random choice each time the template is rendered.

from_fact: random filter was constant-folded (same value each render)
random filter produces a new random choice each render

subject: variable assignment in inner scopes when variable is also read from outer scope
old: limited assignments to outer scopes was possible
new: this now sets the intended behavior in all situations however it does not restore the old behavior where limited assignments to outer scopes was possible
truth: Assigning to a variable in an inner scope that is also read from an outer scope no longer allows limited assignments to outer scopes; the old behavior is not restored.
quote: Set a consistent behavior for assigning of variables in inner scopes when the variable is also read from an outer scope. This now sets the intended behavior in all situations however it does not restore the old behavior where limited assignments to outer scopes was possible.

from_fact: limited assignments to outer scopes was possible
this now sets the intended behavior in all situations however it does not restore the old behavior where limited assignments to outer scopes was possible
associative: modify outer variable from inner block

subject: operator precedence of math operations
old: math operator precedence was what users expected
new: math operator precedence now follows standard mathematical rules
truth: Math operator precedence has been corrected; existing templates relying on the old precedence may silently produce different results.
quote: Corrected a long standing issue with operator precedence of math operations not being what was expected.

from_fact: math operator precedence was what users expected
math operator precedence now follows standard mathematical rules

subject: Urlize filter
old: Urlize did not set rel noopener
new: Urlize now sets rel noopener by default
truth: Urlize now sets rel="noopener" by default on generated links, which may affect link behavior in browsers.
quote: Urlize now sets "rel noopener" by default.

from_fact: urlize did not set rel noopener
urlize now sets rel noopener by default

subject: macro autoescaping logic
old: macro autoescaping was based on macro define time
new: macro autoescaping is based on runtime autoescaping at call time
truth: Macro autoescaping now uses the runtime autoescaping setting at call time instead of at macro definition time, which may change escaping behavior of existing macros.
quote: Change the logic for macro autoescaping to be based on the runtime autoescaping information at call time instead of macro define time.

from_fact: macro autoescaping was based on macro define time
macro autoescaping is based on runtime autoescaping at call time

subject: tests first argument
old: tests accepted an arbitrary expression as first argument
new: tests now accept only a restricted expression as first argument
truth: Tests no longer accept an arbitrary expression as the first argument; you must now use parentheses around complex expressions in tests.
quote: Tests now no longer accept an arbitrary expression as first argument

from_fact: tests accepted an arbitrary expression as first argument
tests now accept only a restricted expression as first argument

subject: scoping system
old: scoping had magic differences between include and import constructs
new: scoping is now consistent; context is always propagated the same way
truth: The scoping system has been overhauled so context propagation is consistent across include and import; existing templates relying on the old scoping differences may break.
quote: Context is now always propagated the same way.

from_fact: scoping had magic differences between include and import constructs
scoping is now consistent; context is always propagated the same way

subject: subtraction on undefined values
old: subtraction on undefined values behaved differently from other operators
new: subtraction now follows the same behavior as other operators on undefined values
truth: Subtraction on undefined values now behaves consistently with other operators, which may change how undefined arithmetic is handled in templates.
quote: Subtraction now follows the same behavior as other operators on undefined values.

from_fact: subtraction on undefined values behaved differently from other operators
subtraction now follows the same behavior as other operators on undefined values

subject: truncate filter
old: truncate filter did not count length
new: truncate filter now counts the length
truth: The truncate filter now counts the length of the truncated string, which may change output for existing templates.
quote: The truncate filter now counts the length.

from_fact: truncate filter did not count length
truncate filter now counts the length

subject: macro default arguments followed by non-default
old: macros allowed default arguments before non-default arguments (e.g. `{% macro m(x, y=1, z) %}`) with broken behavior
new: macros with default arguments followed by non-default arguments are now a syntax error
truth: Macros with default arguments followed by non-default arguments (e.g. `{% macro m(x, y=1, z) %}`) now raise a syntax error instead of silently applying the default to the wrong parameter.
quote: Added a check for default arguments followed by non-default arguments. This change makes `{% macro m(x, y=1, z) %}` a syntax error. The previous behavior for this code was broken anyway (resulting in the default value being applied to `y`).

from_fact: macros allowed default arguments before non-default arguments (e.g. `{% macro m(x, y=1, z) %}`) with broken behavior
macros with default arguments followed by non-default arguments are now a syntax error

subject: cache keys
new: cache keys use absolute file names if available
truth: Cache keys now use absolute file names instead of load names, which invalidates all previously cached templates.
quote: Changed cache keys to use absolute file names if available instead of load names.

from_fact: cache keys use absolute file names if available

subject: default cache size
new: default cache size is 400
truth: The default template cache size increased from 50 to 400, which increases memory usage.
quote: Default cache size was increased to 400 from a low 50.

subject: urlencode escaping of slashes
old: slashes were not escaped in query strings for urlencode
new: slashes are now escaped in query strings for urlencode
truth: The urlencode filter now escapes slashes in query strings, which changes the output for URLs containing slashes.
quote: Escape query strings for urlencode properly. Previously slashes were not escaped in that place.

from_fact: slashes were not escaped in query strings for urlencode
slashes are now escaped in query strings for urlencode

subject: is number test
old: is number test did not accept Decimal
new: is number test now accepts Decimal as a number
truth: The `is number` test now accepts Decimal values as numbers, which may change behavior for code that relied on Decimal not being a number.
quote: Changed `is number` to accept Decimal as a number.

from_fact: is number test did not accept decimal
is number test now accepts decimal as a number

subject: sum filter
old: sum filter's first argument was the optional starting index (defaults to zero)
new: sum filter's first argument is now the attribute to sum by, starting index becomes the second argument
truth: The sum filter's argument order changed: the attribute name to sum by is now the first argument, and the optional starting index is the second argument.
quote: The sum filter can now sum up values by attribute. This is a backwards incompatible change. The argument to the filter previously was the optional starting index which defaults to zero. This now became the second argument to the function because it's rarely used.

from_fact: sum filter's first argument was the optional starting index (defaults to zero)
sum filter's first argument is now the attribute to sum by, starting index becomes the second argument

subject: Python version requirements
truth: Python 2.5 is no longer supported; Python 3.0, 3.1, 3.2 are no longer supported; minimum Python 3 version is now 3.3.
quote: Python requirement changed: 2.6, 2.7 or >= 3.3 are required now, supported by same source code, using the "six" compatibility library.

subject: builtin markup class
truth: Stop using the builtin markup class; switch to markupsafe.
quote: Removed builtin markup class and switched to markupsafe.

subject: StopIteration handling in templates
old: StopIteration exceptions raised by functions called from templates propagated as errors
new: StopIteration exceptions raised by functions called from templates are intercepted and converted to undefineds
truth: StopIteration exceptions raised by functions called from templates are now intercepted and converted to undefined values instead of propagating.
quote: StopIteration exceptions raised by functions called from templates are now intercepted and converted to undefineds.

from_fact: stopiteration exceptions raised by functions called from templates propagated as errors
stopiteration exceptions raised by functions called from templates are intercepted and converted to undefineds

subject: operator precedence for unary operators
old: unary operators like '-' had wrong precedence (e.g. '-foo.bar' parsed as '(-foo).bar')
new: unary operators now have correct precedence (e.g. '-foo.bar' parses as '-(foo.bar)')
truth: Unary operator precedence was fixed: '-foo.bar' now evaluates as '-(foo.bar)' instead of '(-foo).bar', which may silently change expression results.
quote: Fixed an operator precedence error introduced in 2.5.2. Statements like "-foo.bar" had their implicit parentheses applied around the first part of the expression ("(-foo).bar") instead of the more correct "-(foo.bar)".

from_fact: unary operators like '-' had wrong precedence (e.g. '-foo.bar' parsed as '(-foo).bar')
unary operators now have correct precedence (e.g. '-foo.bar' parses as '-(foo.bar)')

subject: implicit tuple expressions
old: empty implicit tuple expressions were allowed (e.g. `{% if %}`)
new: empty implicit tuple expressions are now a syntax error
truth: Implicit tuple expressions can no longer be totally empty; `{% if %}` is now a syntax error.
quote: Implicit tuple expressions can no longer be totally empty.

from_fact: empty implicit tuple expressions were allowed (e.g. `{% if %}`)
empty implicit tuple expressions are now a syntax error

subject: priority of `not` operator
old: `not` had lower precedence, requiring parentheses: `not (foo in bar)`
new: `not` has higher precedence, allowing `not foo in bar` as alias for `foo not in bar`
truth: Priority of `not` was raised; `not foo in bar` now works without parentheses, which may change evaluation order in existing expressions.
quote: like "-foo.bar" had their implicit parentheses applied around the

from_fact: `not` had lower precedence, requiring parentheses: `not (foo in bar)`
`not` has higher precedence, allowing `not foo in bar` as alias for `foo not in bar`

subject: environment attribute 'overlay'
new: attribute renamed to 'overlayed'
truth: The undocumented attribute 'overlay' on the environment was renamed to 'overlayed' because it clashed with a method of the same name.
quote: Renamed (undocumented) attribute "overlay" to "overlayed" on the environment because it was clashing with a method of the same name.

subject: speedup extension
old: speedup extension was enabled by default
new: speedup extension is now disabled by default
truth: The speedup extension is now disabled by default, so templates relying on its behavior will silently run without it.
quote: Speedup extension is now disabled by default.

from_fact: speedup extension was enabled by default
speedup extension is now disabled by default

subject: Index-based subscripting of variables with constant value
new: returns an undefined object
truth: Index-based subscripting of variables with a constant value now returns an undefined object instead of raising an index error.
quote: Index based subscribing of variables with a constant value returns an undefined object now instead of raising an index error.

subject: Subscript AST node
old: Subscript (dot-notation and bracket syntax had fixed priority)
new: Getitem and Getattr (priority can be assigned to attributes or items)
truth: Subscript is gone and replaced with Getitem and Getattr; dot-notation or bracket syntax can now give attributes or items higher priority.
quote: higher priority by either using dot-notation lookup or the bracket

from_fact: subscript (dot-notation and bracket syntax had fixed priority)
getitem and getattr (priority can be assigned to attributes or items)

subject: jinja2.__version__
new: importlib.metadata.version("jinja2")
truth: Stop using jinja2.__version__; use importlib.metadata.version("jinja2") instead.
quote: ``importlib.metadata.version("jinja2")`` instead.

subject: |attr filter sandbox bypass
old: The |attr filter bypassed the environment's attribute lookup checks
new: The |attr filter now respects the sandbox's attribute lookup checks
truth: The |attr filter no longer bypasses the sandbox; attribute access that was previously allowed may now be blocked.
quote: The ``|attr`` filter does not bypass the environment's attribute lookup,

from_fact: the |attr filter bypassed the environment's attribute lookup checks
the |attr filter now respects the sandbox's attribute lookup checks

subject: Sandbox mutable sequence methods
old: Sandbox allowed clear and pop on known mutable sequence types
new: Sandbox now blocks clear and pop on known mutable sequence types
truth: The sandbox no longer allows clear() and pop() on known mutable sequence types; code relying on these will raise an error.
quote: Sandbox does not allow ``clear`` and ``pop`` on known mutable sequence

from_fact: sandbox allowed clear and pop on known mutable sequence types
sandbox now blocks clear and pop on known mutable sequence types

subject: Async template sync render
old: Calling sync render() on an async template used a different mechanism
new: Calling sync render() on an async template now uses asyncio.run
truth: Calling sync render() for an async template now uses asyncio.run, which may change event loop behavior in existing async applications.
quote: Calling sync ``render`` for an async template uses ``asyncio.run``.

from_fact: calling sync render() on an async template used a different mechanism
calling sync render() on an async template now uses asyncio.run

subject: Template.generate_async return type
new: Template.generate_async returns an aclose-able AsyncGenerator
truth: Template.generate_async now returns an AsyncGenerator that must be properly closed with aclose(); old code that didn't close the generator may leak resources.
quote: Return an ``aclose``-able ``AsyncGenerator`` from

subject: Build backend
new: flit_core as build backend
truth: Jinja2 3.2.0 uses flit_core instead of setuptools as build backend; custom builds or packaging workflows relying on setuptools may break.
quote: Use ``flit_core`` instead of ``setuptools`` as build backend.

subject: xmlattr filter
new: does not allow keys with / solidus, > greater-than sign, or = equals sign
truth: The xmlattr filter now rejects keys containing /, >, or = characters in addition to spaces.
quote: The ``xmlattr`` filter does not allow keys with ``/`` solidus, ``>``

from_fact: does not allow keys with / solidus, > greater-than sign, or = equals sign

subject: contextfilter / contextfunction decorators
new: pass_context
truth: Replace contextfilter and contextfunction with pass_context.
quote: ``contextfilter`` and ``contextfunction`` are replaced by

subject: evalcontextfilter / evalcontextfunction decorators
new: pass_eval_context
truth: Replace evalcontextfilter and evalcontextfunction with pass_eval_context.
quote: ``evalcontextfunction`` are replaced by ``pass_eval_context``.

subject: environmentfilter / environmentfunction decorators
new: pass_environment
truth: Replace environmentfilter and environmentfunction with pass_environment.
quote: ``environmentfilter`` and ``environmentfunction`` are replaced

subject: Markup and escape imports
old: importing Markup and escape from Jinja2
new: import from MarkupSafe
truth: Markup and escape must now be imported from MarkupSafe instead of Jinja2.
quote: ``Markup`` and ``escape`` should be imported from MarkupSafe.

from_fact: importing markup and escape from jinja2

subject: Context subclass resolve method
new: override resolve_or_missing instead of resolve
truth: Context subclasses must override resolve_or_missing instead of resolve; legacy resolve mode is no longer supported.
quote: Legacy resolve mode for ``Context`` subclasses is no longer

subject: unicode_urlencode function
new: url_quote
truth: unicode_urlencode has been renamed to url_quote.
quote: ``unicode_urlencode`` is renamed to ``url_quote``.

subject: xmlattr filter key validation (spaces)
new: does not allow keys with spaces
truth: The xmlattr filter does not allow keys with spaces.
quote: ``xmlattr`` filter does not allow keys with spaces.

from_fact: does not allow keys with spaces

subject: PackageLoader path handling
old: PackageLoader included a current directory (.) path segment
new: PackageLoader will not include a current directory (.) path segment
truth: Stop relying on PackageLoader including a '.' path segment; template loading from zip imports may now resolve differently.
quote: ``PackageLoader`` will not include a current directory (.) path

from_fact: packageloader included a current directory (.) path segment
packageloader will not include a current directory (.) path segment

subject: jinja2.Markup called without argument
new: markupsafe.Markup()
truth: Stop using jinja2.Markup() without an argument; use markupsafe.Markup() instead.
quote: Fixed calling deprecated ``jinja2.Markup`` without an argument.

subject: Undefined.__contains__ behavior
new: Undefined.__contains__ raises UndefinedError
truth: Using 'in' on an Undefined value now raises UndefinedError instead of TypeError.
quote: ``Undefined.__contains__`` (``in``) raises an ``UndefinedError``

subject: URL scheme default in urlize filter
old: URLs without scheme linked as http://
new: URLs without scheme linked as https://
truth: The urlize filter now links URLs without a scheme as https:// instead of http://.
quote: without a scheme are linked as ``https://`` instead of ``http://``.

from_fact: urls without scheme linked as http://
urls without scheme linked as https://

subject: Line break handling in template rendering
old: Other line break characters were treated as breaks
new: Only \n, \r\n, \r are line breaks; other characters unchanged
truth: Template rendering now only treats \n, \r\n, and \r as line breaks; other characters are left unchanged.
quote: Template rendering only treats ``\n``, ``\r\n`` and ``\r`` as line

from_fact: other line break characters were treated as breaks
only \n, \r\n, \r are line breaks; other characters unchanged

subject: autoescape and with extensions
old: autoescape and with were separate extensions
new: autoescape and with are deprecated; built into compiler
truth: The autoescape and with extensions are deprecated; they are now built into the compiler.
quote: Deprecate the ``autoescape`` and ``with`` extensions, they are

from_fact: autoescape and with were separate extensions
autoescape and with are deprecated; built into compiler

subject: contextfunction / contextfilter
new: pass_context
truth: Stop using contextfunction and contextfilter; use pass_context instead.
quote: ``contextfilter`` and ``contextfunction`` are replaced by

subject: evalcontextfunction / evalcontextfilter
new: pass_eval_context
truth: Stop using evalcontextfunction and evalcontextfilter; use pass_eval_context instead.
quote: ``evalcontextfunction`` are replaced by ``pass_eval_context``.

subject: environmentfunction / environmentfilter
new: pass_environment
truth: Stop using environmentfunction and environmentfilter; use pass_environment instead.
quote: ``environmentfilter`` and ``environmentfunction`` are replaced

subject: Context.resolve
new: resolve_or_missing
truth: Stop overriding Context.resolve; override resolve_or_missing instead.
quote: Overriding ``Context.resolve`` is deprecated, override

subject: import statement
new: import statement cannot end with a trailing comma
truth: Stop using trailing commas in import statements; they are no longer allowed.
quote: ``import`` statement cannot end with a trailing comma.

from_fact: import statement cannot end with a trailing comma

subject: indent filter
new: indent filter does not indent blank lines by default
truth: The indent filter no longer indents blank lines by default; existing templates relying on blank lines being indented will produce different output.
quote: ``indent`` filter will not indent blank lines by default.

from_fact: indent filter does not indent blank lines by default

subject: block sets
old: block sets were not marked safe
new: block sets are now marked safe by default
truth: Block sets are now marked as safe by default, which may cause double-escaping or missing escaping in templates that manually escaped them.
quote: Block sets are now marked ``safe`` by default.

from_fact: block sets were not marked safe
block sets are now marked safe by default

subject: filesizeformat filter
new: uses decimal prefixes by default, second parameter to switch to binary
truth: filesizeformat filter now uses decimal prefixes by default; pass a second parameter to use binary mode.
quote: ``filesizeformat`` filter uses decimal prefixes now by default and

from_fact: uses decimal prefixes by default, second parameter to switch to binary

subject: Conditional expressions without else
new: else is optional, evaluates to false
truth: Else is now optional for conditional expressions; if omitted it evaluates to false.
quote: ``else`` is optional for conditional expressions.

from_fact: else is optional, evaluates to false
